Author: Aeschylus
Greek_Text_Snippet: φάρμακα χριστά τε καὶ πιστά
English_Translation: ...smearable medicines and drinkable medicines [which free men from illnesses]
Lexicon_Note: LSJ I: 'to be rubbed on, used as ointment or salve, opp. πιστός' (Aesch. Pr.480)

Author: Euripides
Greek_Text_Snippet: πότερα δὲ χριστὸν ἢ ποτὸν τὸ φάρμακον;
English_Translation: Is the drug a smearable [salve] or a drinkable [potion]?
Lexicon_Note: LSJ I: same entry; contrasted with ποτόν. Technical medical vocab.

Author: Hippocrates (Corpus)
Greek_Text_Snippet: τοῖσι δὲ ἐν θέρει καὶ χειμῶνι ἐλαίῳ χρηστέον ἐπὶ τὰ ἕλκεα
English_Translation: In summer and winter one must apply oil to ulcers that require such medicines.
Lexicon_Note: Hippocratic corpus is primary locus of χριστά = topical medications (contrast πιστά). Not in LSJ s.v. χριστός separately but cognate entries confirm.

Author: Septuagint — Leviticus
Greek_Text_Snippet: ὁ ἀρχιερεὺς ὁ κεχρισμένος ἁμάρτῃ τοῦ τὸν λαὸν ἁμαρτεῖν
English_Translation: …if the anointed high priest sins so as to bring guilt on the people
Lexicon_Note: LSJ IIa: 'anointed [priestly]… LXX Lev 4.5, 4.16, 6.22'

Author: Septuagint — Leviticus
Greek_Text_Snippet: ὁ ἱερεὺς ὁ χριστὸς ὁ τετελειωμένος τὰς χεῖρας
English_Translation: the anointed priest who has been ordained [shall take the blood of the bull]
Lexicon_Note: LSJ IIa. ὁ χριστός used as frozen title for the anointed (high) priest.

Author: Septuagint — Leviticus
Greek_Text_Snippet: ὁ ἱερεὺς ὁ χριστὸς εἰσοίσει ἀπὸ τοῦ αἵματος τοῦ μόσχου
English_Translation: the anointed priest shall bring some of the blood of the bull
Lexicon_Note: LSJ IIa.

Author: Septuagint — Leviticus
Greek_Text_Snippet: ὁ ἱερεὺς ὁ χριστὸς ἀντ' αὐτοῦ ἐκ τῶν υἱῶν αὐτοῦ ποιήσει αὐτήν
English_Translation: the anointed priest who succeeds him from among his sons shall perform it
Lexicon_Note: LSJ IIa.

Author: Septuagint — 1 Samuel
Greek_Text_Snippet: κύριος δώσει ἰσχὺν τοῖς βασιλεῦσιν ἡμῶν καὶ ὑψώσει κέρας χριστοῦ αὐτοῦ
English_Translation: the LORD will give strength to our kings and will exalt the horn of his anointed
Lexicon_Note: LSJ IIb: 'Kings of Israel'. Hannah's prayer; prospective reference to anointed king.

Author: Septuagint — 1 Samuel
Grammatical_Case: Dat. sg. (1st use); Nom. sg. pred. (2nd use)
Greek_Text_Snippet: τῷ χριστῷ κυρίου … ὅτι χριστὸς κυρίου ἐστὶν οὗτος
English_Translation: to the LORD's anointed … because this is the LORD's anointed
Lexicon_Note: LSJ IIb. David refuses to harm Saul as χριστὸς κυρίου — the paradigmatic use of the formula.

Author: Septuagint — Psalms
Greek_Text_Snippet: παρέστησαν οἱ βασιλεῖς τῆς γῆς … κατὰ τοῦ κυρίου καὶ κατὰ τοῦ χριστοῦ αὐτοῦ
English_Translation: The kings of the earth took their stand … against the LORD and against his anointed
Lexicon_Note: Most-cited LXX messianic verse; applied to Jesus in Acts 4:26. LSJ IIb.

Author: Septuagint — Psalms
Greek_Text_Snippet: ποιῶν ἔλεος τῷ χριστῷ αὐτοῦ τῷ Δαυὶδ καὶ τῷ σπέρματι αὐτοῦ ἕως αἰῶνος
English_Translation: showing loving-kindness to his anointed — to David and to his seed forever
Lexicon_Note: LSJ IIb explicitly cites this verse. Davidic covenant context.

Author: Septuagint — Psalms
Greek_Text_Snippet: ἀνεβάλου τὸν χριστόν σου
English_Translation: you have spurned your anointed
Lexicon_Note: Lament over abandoned Davidic covenant. LSJ IIb type.

Author: Septuagint — Psalms
Greek_Text_Snippet: οὗ ὠνείδισαν τὴν ἀνταλλαγὴν τοῦ χριστοῦ σου
English_Translation: who have reproached the footsteps of your anointed
Lexicon_Note: LSJ IIb.

Author: Septuagint — Psalms
Greek_Text_Snippet: μὴ ἀποστρέψῃς τὸ πρόσωπον τοῦ χριστοῦ σου
English_Translation: do not turn away the face of your anointed
Lexicon_Note: Davidic covenant; cited in early Christian exegesis.

Author: Septuagint — Psalms
Greek_Text_Snippet: ἡτοίμασα λύχνον τῷ χριστῷ μου
English_Translation: I have prepared a lamp for my anointed
Lexicon_Note: Davidic dynasty promise. LSJ IIb.

Author: Septuagint — Isaiah
Greek_Text_Snippet: Οὕτως λέγει κύριος ὁ θεὸς τῷ χριστῷ μου Κύρῳ
English_Translation: Thus says the Lord God to my anointed — Cyrus
Lexicon_Note: Only LXX text where χριστός is applied to a non-Israelite (Cyrus II of Persia). LSJ IIb extended.

Author: Septuagint — Habakkuk
Greek_Text_Snippet: ἐξήλθες εἰς σωτηρίαν λαοῦ σου τοῦ σῷσαι τοὺς χριστούς σου
English_Translation: You went out for the salvation of your people to save your anointed ones
Lexicon_Note: Only pl. acc. occurrence of χριστός in the LXX noted in LSJ. Collective royal/national usage.

Author: Septuagint — Lamentations
Greek_Text_Snippet: πνεῦμα προσώπου ἡμῶν χριστὸς κυρίου συνελήμφθη ἐν ταῖς διαφθοραῖς αὐτῶν
English_Translation: The breath of our nostrils — the LORD's anointed — was caught in their traps
Lexicon_Note: Formula χριστὸς κυρίου mourning the captured king (Zedekiah). LSJ IIb.

Author: Septuagint — Daniel (Theodotion)
Greek_Text_Snippet: ἕως χριστοῦ ἡγουμένου ἑβδομάδες ἑπτὰ
English_Translation: until an anointed leader [there shall be] seven weeks
Lexicon_Note: 'Seventy weeks' prophecy; foundational for Second Temple messianic chronology.

Author: Septuagint — Daniel (Theodotion)
Greek_Text_Snippet: ἐξολοθρευθήσεται χριστός καὶ κρίμα οὐκ ἔστιν ἐν αὐτῷ
English_Translation: an anointed one will be cut off and will have nothing
Lexicon_Note: LXX Vorlage differs (has χρῖσμα not χριστός); Theodotion's χριστός becomes standard in Christian citation.

Author: Flavius Josephus
Greek_Text_Snippet: ὁ χριστὸς οὗτος ἦν
English_Translation: He was the Christ
Lexicon_Note: Testimonium Flavianum — authenticity debated. ὁ χριστὸς suggests Christian interpolation in current form.

Author: Flavius Josephus
Greek_Text_Snippet: τὸν ἀδελφὸν Ἰησοῦ τοῦ λεγομένου Χριστοῦ Ἰάκωβος ὄνομα αὐτῷ
English_Translation: the brother of Jesus who was called Christ — James by name
Lexicon_Note: τοῦ λεγομένου Χριστοῦ = distancing formula; generally considered authentic. Most important non-Christian attestation.

Author: Matthew
Greek_Text_Snippet: Βίβλος γενέσεως Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ υἱοῦ Δαυὶδ
English_Translation: The book of the genealogy of Jesus Christ son of David
Lexicon_Note: Without article: proper name usage. LSJ III: 'a proper name for Jesus' (Matt 1:1). First word of Gospel.

Author: Matthew
Greek_Text_Snippet: ἐπυνθάνετο παρ' αὐτῶν ποῦ ὁ Χριστὸς γεννᾶται
English_Translation: he inquired of them where the Christ was to be born
Lexicon_Note: With article: definite title. Herod's inquiry implies widespread public use of the messianic title.

Author: Matthew
Greek_Text_Snippet: Σὺ εἶ ὁ Χριστὸς ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ θεοῦ τοῦ ζῶντος
English_Translation: You are the Christ the Son of the living God
Lexicon_Note: Peter's confession — climactic identification of Jesus as χριστός. LSJ III.

Author: Mark
Greek_Text_Snippet: Ἀρχὴ τοῦ εὐαγγελίου Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ υἱοῦ θεοῦ
English_Translation: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ Son of God
Lexicon_Note: Incipit; Χριστός without article = proper name. Earliest canonical gospel.

Author: Luke
Greek_Text_Snippet: ὅς ἐστιν Χριστὸς κύριος
English_Translation: who is Christ the Lord
Lexicon_Note: Two titles combined: Χριστός + κύριος. Angel's announcement at nativity. LSJ III cites Luke 2:26.

Author: John
Greek_Text_Snippet: Εὑρήκαμεν τὸν Μεσσίαν ὅ ἐστιν μεθερμηνευόμενον ὁ Χριστός
English_Translation: We have found the Messiah (which is translated: the Christ)
Lexicon_Note: Only NT passage with explicit Greek-Aramaic bilingual equation: Μεσσίας = ὁ Χριστός. LSJ III.

Author: John
Semantic_Category: Christian confessional title (stated purpose of Gospel)
Greek_Text_Snippet: ἵνα πιστεύ[σ]ητε ὅτι Ἰησοῦς ἐστιν ὁ Χριστὸς ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ θεοῦ
English_Translation: that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ the Son of God
Lexicon_Note: Gospel's purpose statement. ὁ Χριστός = definite title. LSJ III.

Author: Paul of Tarsus
Greek_Text_Snippet: δοῦλος Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ
English_Translation: a slave of Jesus Christ
Lexicon_Note: Pauline letter opening formula. Χριστός without article = proper name. ~36 occurrences in Romans.

Author: Paul of Tarsus
Greek_Text_Snippet: ἡμεῖς δὲ κηρύσσομεν Χριστὸν ἐσταυρωμένον Ἰουδαίοις μὲν σκάνδαλον
English_Translation: …but we preach Christ crucified — a stumbling block to Jews
Lexicon_Note: Χριστός here functions as proper name but retains titular force. ~65 occurrences in 1 Cor.

Author: Paul of Tarsus
Greek_Text_Snippet: Χριστὸς ἀπέθανεν ὑπὲρ τῶν ἁμαρτιῶν ἡμῶν κατὰ τὰς γραφάς
English_Translation: Christ died for our sins according to the scriptures
Lexicon_Note: Pre-Pauline creedal formula (c.35-40 CE). Earliest dateable Christian creed. Χριστός as proper name.

Author: Paul of Tarsus
Grammatical_Case: Masc. sg. dat. (locative phrase ἐν Χριστῷ)
Greek_Text_Snippet: εἷς ἐστε ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ
English_Translation: you are all one in Christ Jesus
Lexicon_Note: Pauline 'in Christ' (ἐν Χριστῷ) formula — ~164 occurrences of this phrase across Pauline corpus.

Author: Paul of Tarsus
Greek_Text_Snippet: ἐμοὶ γὰρ τὸ ζῆν Χριστὸς καὶ τὸ ἀποθανεῖν κέρδος
English_Translation: For to me to live is Christ and to die is gain
Lexicon_Note: Χριστός as the content of life itself — proper name at maximum integration with speaker's identity.

Author: Acts of the Apostles
Greek_Text_Snippet: καὶ Κύριον αὐτὸν καὶ Χριστὸν ἐποίησεν ὁ θεός τοῦτον τὸν Ἰησοῦν
English_Translation: God has made him both Lord and Christ — this Jesus
Lexicon_Note: God as subject who makes Jesus Χριστός — theological claim about messianic status. ~27 occ. in Acts.

Author: Acts of the Apostles
English_Translation: ἐχρημάτισάν τε πρῶτον ἐν Ἀντιοχείᾳ τοὺς μαθητὰς Χριστιανούς
Lexicon_Note: …and the disciples were first called Christians in Antioch
Source_Reference: Χριστιανοί: derivative showing Χριστός had become a proper name by which followers could be named (c.40-50 CE).

Author: Revelation (Apocalypse)
Greek_Text_Snippet: ἐγένετο ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ κόσμου τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν καὶ τοῦ Χριστοῦ αὐτοῦ
English_Translation: The kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord and of his Christ
Lexicon_Note: Both κύριος and Χριστός retain full titular force in apocalyptic context. ~7 occ. in Rev.

Author: Hebrews (epistle)
Greek_Text_Snippet: Χριστὸς δὲ παραγενόμενος ἀρχιερεὺς τῶν γενομένων ἀγαθῶν
English_Translation: …but Christ, having appeared as high priest of the good things that have come
Lexicon_Note: Christological reinterpretation: Χριστός as the 'anointed' priestly figure of Lev 4 — connecting back to original LXX priestly usage. ~12 occ. in Hebrews.
